drmd:description.dcc:content: The Reference Material is available in the form of discs (approx. 40 mm diameter and 40 mm height).

dcc:description.dcc:content: As: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF. Bi: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate. Sb: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF. Sn: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate; FAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF. Ag: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; FAAS, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate. Cd: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate; ETAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF. Cu: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ETAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate. Ni: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ETAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate. Te: ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ETAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF. Tl: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ETAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-MS, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate. Zn: ICP-OES, dissolution with tartaric acid/HNO3; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/HF; FAAS, dissolution with HNO3/HF; ICP-OES, dissolution with HNO3/tetraborate.

drmd:description.dcc:content: 1) Unweighted mean value of the means of accepted sets of data, each set being obtained by at least 4 laboratories and/or with different methods of measurement. The values are traceable to the SI (Système International d'Unités) by the use of pure substances of known stoichiometry for calibration. 2) Estimated expanded uncertainty U with a coverage factor of k = 2, corresponding to a level of confidence of approx. 95%, as defined in the Guide to the expression of uncertainty in measurement, (GUM, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008).

drmd:intendedUse.dcc:content: The CRM is intended for establishing or checking the calibration of spark optical emission spectrometers for the analysis of samples of similar matrix composition. The minimum sample size for wet chemical analysis is 0.1 g.

drmd:storageInformation.dcc:content: The material should be stored in a dry and clean environment at room temperature. Transport under normal ambient conditions.

drmd:instructionsForHandlingAndUse.dcc:content: Before use, the surface of the material must be prepared by milling or turning on a lathe. For wet chemical analysis chips have to be prepared by turning or milling of the sample surface. An area 8mm in diameter in the centre of the discs should be avoided for spark optical emission spectrometry.

drmd:metrologicalTraceability.dcc:content: The values are traceable to the SI (Système International d'Unités) via calibration using pure metals or substances of known stoichiometry. All values were confirmed in an inter-laboratory comparison using spark optical emission spectrometry.

drmd:subcontractors.dcc:content: Aurubis AG, Hamburg, Germany Bundesanstalt für Materialforschung und -prüfung (BAM), Berlin, Germany Berzelius Stolberg, Stolberg, Germany Hoppecke Batterien GmbH & Co. KG, Brilon-Hoppecke, Germany Johnson Controls Sachsen-Batterien GmbH & Co. KG, Zwickau, Germany Johnson Controls, VB Autobatterie GmbH & Co. KGaA, Hannover, Germany Muldenhütten Recycling und Umwelttechnik GmbH, Freiberg, Germany Recyclex Harz-Metall GmbH, Goslar, Germany TAB-IPM d.o.o., Žerjav, Slovenia ThyssenKrupp Steel Europe, Duisburg, Germany WESER METALL GmbH, Nordenham, Germany

drmd:referenceToCertificationReport.dcc:content: A detailed technical report describing the analysis procedures and the treatment of the analytical data used to certify BAM-M109 is available on request or can be downloaded from BAM website (www.bam.de).

drmd:digitalReferenceMaterialDocument.drmd:comment: For additional information please refer to the pdf certificate
